name: importStudy
description: Describe how to import a JATOS study by uploading a study archive (.jzip) via the JATOS HTTP API. This tool does not perform the upload itself. Instead, it explains how to construct a multipart/form-data POST request to the JATOS endpoint that accepts a study archive file and imports it, optionally controlling how existing studies and assets are handled.

filePath.description: Local path to the JATOS study archive file (.jzip) that should be uploaded.

keepProperties.description: If a study with the same UUID already exists, keep its current properties instead of overwriting them.

keepAssets.description: If a study with the same UUID already exists, keep its current assets directory instead of overwriting it.

keepCurrentAssetsName.description: When assets are overwritten, keep the existing assets directory name instead of using the uploaded one.

renameAssets.description: If the uploaded assets directory name conflicts with another study, allow JATOS to rename it by appending a numeric suffix.

success.description: True if the upload instruction generation succeeded.

message.description: A message that describes the result of the operation.

curlCommand.description: A fully-formed curl command to upload the study archive file (.jzip) and import it in JATOS. Copy and paste into a terminal.

importUrl.description: The URL to import the study archive to the JATOS server.

result.description: Metadata returned by JATOS after importing the study via the API.

uuid.description: The UUID identifier of the imported study.

studyExists.description: Whether a study with the same UUID already existed before the import.

currentStudyTitle.description: The previous study title, if it was overwritten.

currentDirName.description: The previous assets directory name, if it was overwritten.

uploadedStudyTitle.description: The title of the uploaded study.

uploadedDirName.description: The assets directory name contained in the uploaded archive.

uploadedDirExists.description: Whether the uploaded assets directory already existed before the import.

newDirName.description: The final assets directory name used if renaming was required.
